_doc: 12 calamity cards (rules §9, §30). `level` = trade-card stack the calamity is shuffled/placed in (= severity tier), from §30 numbering and the card SVG filenames. `tradable`: tradable calamities are shuffled into commodity stacks and can be passed via trade; non-tradable are drawn separately and cannot be traded away. `severity` is the calamity's resolution order rank (higher = resolved later / worse). `effect` captures the core magnitude for the engine; elaborate procedures (Civil War factions, Barbarian Hordes movement, Piracy pirate-cities) are summarized and implemented in the engine. `worsenedBy`/`reducedBy`/`nullifiedBy` list civilization advances that modify the effect (rules §30 cross-refs and §32).
tradableNote: 8 tradable / 4 non-tradable. VERIFIED against the rules §9.1 table (read from the rendered rulebook page 6). NON-TRADABLE (4): Volcano/Earthquake, Famine, Civil War, Flood. TRADABLE (8): Treachery, Superstition, Slave Revolt, Barbarian Hordes, Epidemic, Civil Disorder, Iconoclasm & Heresy, Piracy.

effect.note: All units in the struck area destroyed; if a city, the city is lost (§30.211-213).
One of the primary victim's cities is taken over by another player; if not traded, attacker logic applies (§30.22).
§30.32; 2 cities if victim holds Mysticism per §30.322 nuance.
§30.41. Primary victim splits off a faction (15 unit pts, +5 with Music/Drama) ceded to another player.
§30.42. Cities reduced; freed tokens cannot be used for city support this turn.
§30.51. Units on a flood plain lost (min 10 unit pts).
§30.52. 15 barbarian tokens placed and move attacking nearby nations. Crete is immune as primary victim.
§30.61. Primary -16 (Medicine -8, Roadbuilding +5); primary orders 25 unit points of loss among other players (Medicine -5, Roadbuilding +5; not the trader).
§30.71. All but three of the victim's cities are reduced.

§30.81. Primary 4 cities (Law -1, Philosophy -1, Theology -3, Monotheism +1, Roadbuilding +1, cumulative); primary orders 2 cities reduced among other players (not trader, not Theology-holders; a Philosophy-holder loses <=1).
§30.91. Primary loses 2 coastal cities (→ pirate cities); 2 other players lose 1 coastal city each (secondary victims, not the trader).
